objective: Reviewed CART-S01-T01 implementation (fix mkdirSync static import + verify build/test/lint gates) against PLAN.md, task prompt, and project conventions.
findings: AC#1 SATISFIED: graph.ts:2 is a single static import including mkdirSync from 'fs' (no await import anywhere).
AC#2 SATISFIED: save() (graph.ts:13-17) is synchronous, no await, calls mkdirSync(recursive) before writeFileSync.
AC#3 SATISFIED: re-ran npm run build live -> exit 0, no TS errors; save now exported (graph.ts:65) resolving TS2459.
AC#4 SATISFIED: re-ran npm test live -> exit 0, 6/6 across 2 suites incl CART-B01 mkdirSync-before-writeFileSync regression guard.
AC#5 SATISFIED: re-ran npm run lint live -> exit 0; eslint.config.js + tseslint deps present, lint script wired.
AC#6 SATISFIED: stale graph.ts:save() / await import('fs') known-issues bullet removed from CLAUDE.md.
Architecture aligned: pure functions, no class/singleton, ESM .js imports intact, no network/DB dep.
In-bounds scope: exporting save (needed for test to compile) and adding ESLint (lint gate had no runner) serve the ACs, not creep.
All gates independently re-run by reviewer (not taken on implementer word); results match PROGRESS.md exactly.
Advisory (pre-existing, out of scope): DATA_PATH falls back to literal '~' when HOME unset (graph.ts:6,14) - future hardening candidate.
No security surface: offline CLI, no new untrusted input.
